title: Qatalum initiates controlled shutdown of aluminium production
summary: Hydro's joint venture company Qatalum has initiated a controlled shutdown of its aluminium production due to a shortage of natural gas in Qatar. The shutdown started on March 3 and is expected to be completed by the end of March, with a full restart potentially taking six to twelve months. Hydro has issued a Force Majeure notice to its Qatalum customers and is working to mitigate consequences and fulfill contractual commitments through alternative channels.

title: Aluminum fell to 3,464.50 USD/T on April 7, 2026
summary: Aluminum prices fell 0.12% to $3,464.50 per tonne, though futures in the UK have risen toward $3,500 per tonne, approaching their highest level in more than four years. The price volatility is driven by geopolitical tensions, with Emirates Global Aluminium halting operations at its Al Taweelah smelter after Iranian missile strikes, and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz severely disrupting outbound shipments from the Gulf region, which supplies roughly 9% of global aluminum.

title: Scientists at Oak Ridge National Laboratory Create New Aluminum Alloy RidgeAlloy
summary: Scientists at Oak Ridge National Laboratory have developed a new aluminum alloy called RidgeAlloy that can convert contaminated car-body scrap into usable material. This innovation supports circular economy principles in aluminum manufacturing.

title: New Aluminum-Thermal System Unlocks Carbon-Free Industrial Heat and Hydrogen
summary: A new aluminum-thermal system has been developed that generates carbon-free industrial heat and hydrogen by leveraging aluminum's energy density as a replacement for fossil fuels. This technology represents a significant advancement in decarbonizing industrial processes.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices rose to 3090 CNY/T on April 8, 2026, up 0.06% from the previous day, despite a 0.90% decline over the past month. Steel rebar futures fell below CNY 3,100 per ton to a four-week low amid uncertainties from the Middle East conflict and potential supply disruptions. Investors are monitoring President Donald Trump’s deadline for Iran, alongside reports of revised US tariffs on steel products.

title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: HRC Steel fell to 1,078.06 USD/T on April 7, 2026, down 0.18% from the previous day, though it has risen 3.96% over the past month and 17.18% year-over-year. Prices recently increased to 1075.00 USD/T, the highest since January 2024, with a 2.8% gain over the past four weeks.

title: Daily Steel Prices
summary: SteelOrbis reports daily price levels for global steel products including HMS I/II 80:20 and shredded scrap, with weekly changes up to 7.44% as of April 7, 2026. Prices for HDG and PPGI from China and other regions show modest weekly gains of 2.51% to 6.32%. Data reflects highest, lowest, and average prices across major markets like Turkey and the US.

title: India's Jindal Steel lifts syngas use on propane crunch
summary: Indian producer Jindal Steel has begun using synthesis gas (syngas) at its galvanizing and colour coating units to address natural gas and propane shortages. The company previously used syngas for direct-reduced iron (DRI) production. Market turbulence is also influenced by Mideast Gulf war disruptions and US tariff policy changes following a Supreme Court decision.

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday after a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers. The restart follows recent disruptions in Chilean copper output.

title: Goldman warns downside risk for copper, trims price target
summary: Goldman Sachs has warned of downside risks for copper prices and trimmed its price target. This reflects concerns over global growth and trade tensions affecting demand. The update comes amid recent volatility in industrial metals.

title: Copper Caught Between Trade Tensions and Global Growth
summary: Copper prices are caught between escalating trade tensions and uncertainty over global growth. This positioning highlights risks to industrial metal demand amid macroeconomic pressures. Market participants await key data like US inflation figures.

title: Japanese copper smelters' H1 output seen rising 3.3% y/y
summary: Japanese copper smelters are expected to increase their first-half output by 3.3% year-over-year. Pan Pacific Copper, the largest supplier, anticipates its first-half supply to rise 7.5% from the previous year.

title: Vale advances Oman maintenance shutdowns to counter war impacts
summary: Vale has accelerated maintenance shutdowns at its Oman operations, which have an annual production capacity of 9 million tons of iron ore pellets—roughly 29% of the company's overall output last year. The move is intended to counter impacts from geopolitical tensions.

title: Rio Tinto's Nuton technology produces first copper
summary: Rio Tinto's Nuton technology has produced its first copper at the Johnson Camp mine in Arizona, achieving the lowest mine-to-metal carbon footprint in the U.S. at 0.82 kg CO₂-e per kg copper. The technology enables cleaner, faster production from low-grade ores, with up to 80% less water usage and 60% lower emissions than traditional methods, while extending mine life. It advanced from concept to production in just 18 months, far quicker than industry norms.

title: CMOC Joins the International Copper Association to Advance Sustainable Industry Development
summary: CMOC Group Limited has joined the International Copper Association as its first Chinese member to promote sustainable development in the copper industry. This membership supports advancements in market, policy, mining, energy, and sustainability practices. It highlights growing collaboration for responsible copper production amid rising global demand.

title: Sustainable and Efficient Practices at Atlantic Copper’s Huelva Smelter: Innovations in Recycling of E-Waste
summary: Atlantic Copper, a Freeport-McMoRan subsidiary and ICA member, hosted events showcasing innovations in e-waste recycling at its Huelva smelter. These practices enhance sustainable copper production through efficient recycling methods. The initiatives align with industry efforts to reduce environmental impact while meeting clean energy demands.

title: Nickel Price Update: Rose to 17208.75 USD/T on April 8, 2026
summary: Nickel rose to 17208.75 USD/T on April 8, 2026, up 0.25% from the previous day. Over the past month, prices have fallen 1.27%, but remain 19.34% higher year-over-year. Futures are trading around $17,000 amid structural tightness and shipping disruptions.

title: Nickel Fell to 17,025 USD/T on April 7, 2026
summary: Nickel fell to 17,025 USD/T on April 7, 2026, down 0.12% from the previous day. Monthly decline stands at 2.32%, though prices are 20.32% above last year. Supply constraints from Indonesia's export tax and Strait of Hormuz disruptions add pressure.

title: Nickel Futures Rebound to $17,300 per Tonne Amid Middle East Hopes
summary: Nickel futures rose to around $17,300 per tonne, rebounding from a one-month low, due to hopes for a US-proposed one-month ceasefire with Iran. This boosted risk sentiment and short-term gains in industrial metals. Investor caution persists amid regional tensions.

title: Nickel Industries showcases sustainability in quarterly update
summary: Nickel Industries Limited reported stable operations and zero lost-time injuries in its Quarterly Activities Report, achieving 17.7 million safe man-hours and a low TRIFR of 0.68. The company completed a strategic acquisition of a 10% stake in Excelsior Nickel Cobalt with an offtake agreement. It also received awards for sustainability leadership from Indonesia Leadership Award 2025 and CNBC Indonesia.

title: New Technology Set to Revolutionize Nickel Pollution Cleanup
summary: Albanian Minerals promotes olivine, a natural mineral, for neutralizing acidic nickel-contaminated environments, immobilizing toxic nickel from water and soil, and restoring ecosystems. The technology also captures atmospheric CO₂ through weathering, addressing both nickel pollution and climate change. It supports agriculture by replenishing nutrients like magnesium, iron, and silicon.

title: Prysmian shares rise as Italian cable maker seen as winner amid US copper tariffs
summary: Prysmian shares rose as much as 4% following U.S. President Donald Trump's announcement of a 50% tariff on imported copper. The world's largest cable maker is positioned as a tariff winner because its U.S. low-voltage business sources raw copper locally from an Arizona mine and operates its own copper rod mill in Texas, giving it a competitive advantage over smaller competitors.
